# NSP Access Rule Submodule

This submodule manages access rules for Network Security Perimeter profiles (`Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityPerimeters/profiles/accessRules`).

Access rules define inbound and outbound network access policies for a profile, using address prefixes, FQDNs, subscriptions, service tags, email addresses, or phone numbers.

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD033 -->
## Requirements

The following requirements are needed by this module:

- <a name="requirement_terraform"></a> [terraform](#requirement\_terraform) (>= 1.9, < 2.0)

- <a name="requirement_azapi"></a> [azapi](#requirement\_azapi) (~> 2.4)

## Resources

The following resources are used by this module:

- [azapi_resource.this](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/Azure/azapi/latest/docs/resources/resource) (resource)

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD013 -->
## Required Inputs

The following input variables are required:

### <a name="input_direction"></a> [direction](#input\_direction)

Description: Direction of the access rule. Possible values: `Inbound`, `Outbound`.

Type: `string`

### <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name)

Description: The name of the access rule. Must be 1-80 characters, start and end with alphanumeric, and may contain alphanumeric, underscore, period, or hyphen.

Type: `string`

### <a name="input_profile_resource_id"></a> [profile\_resource\_id](#input\_profile\_resource\_id)

Description: The resource ID of the parent NSP profile.

Type: `string`

## Optional Inputs

The following input variables are optional (have default values):

### <a name="input_address_prefixes"></a> [address\_prefixes](#input\_address\_prefixes)

Description: List of inbound IPv4/IPv6 address prefixes. Applicable for `Inbound` rules.

Type: `list(string)`

Default: `[]`

### <a name="input_email_addresses"></a> [email\_addresses](#input\_email\_addresses)

Description: List of outbound email addresses. Currently unavailable for use.

Type: `list(string)`

Default: `[]`

### <a name="input_fully_qualified_domain_names"></a> [fully\_qualified\_domain\_names](#input\_fully\_qualified\_domain\_names)

Description: List of outbound FQDNs. Applicable for `Outbound` rules.

Type: `list(string)`

Default: `[]`

### <a name="input_phone_numbers"></a> [phone\_numbers](#input\_phone\_numbers)

Description: List of outbound phone numbers. Currently unavailable for use.

Type: `list(string)`

Default: `[]`

### <a name="input_service_tags"></a> [service\_tags](#input\_service\_tags)

Description: List of inbound service tag names. Currently unavailable for use.

Type: `list(string)`

Default: `[]`

### <a name="input_subscriptions"></a> [subscriptions](#input\_subscriptions)

Description: List of subscription IDs (ARM format: `/subscriptions/{id}`) for cross-subscription inbound access.

Type: `list(string)`

Default: `[]`

## Outputs

The following outputs are exported:

### <a name="output_name"></a> [name](#output\_name)

Description: The name of the access rule.

### <a name="output_resource"></a> [resource](#output\_resource)

Description: The full access rule resource object.

### <a name="output_resource_id"></a> [resource\_id](#output\_resource\_id)

Description: The resource ID of the access rule.

## Modules

No modules.

You may turn off the telemetry as described in the repository. There are also some features in the software that may enable you and Microsoft to collect data from users of your applications. Your use of the software operates as your consent to these practices.
